28.code: for (int j = i+1; j < h; j++)
28.reason: Declaring the loop variable 'j' inside the 'for' statement is a C99 feature. It will cause a compilation error if the compiler is set to C89/C90 standard.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Error: When j = h-1, a[j+1] and s[j] access out‑of‑bounds (h is the last index after increment). Also shifting s[j-1] = s[j] incorrectly overwrites operators.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Compilation error in strict C89/C90: declaring loop variable 'j' inside the for initializer is not allowed. Move 'int j;' declaration outside the loop.
